subject: Acolytes only if defending? If Lizards attacks, dead warriors don’t become Acolytes?

posts.body:
Hello Everyone,

We have a major debate about this and it seems the digital inplementations also imdicate fhat Lizard warriors only become Acolytes if they are defending an attack. If the Lizards are the attacker, the dead warriors go back to the supply pile. Is this right?
So if no one wants to attack the Lizards to deny them of Acolytes and they can only train, then it seems the Lizard power is quite weak.

thirdman wrote:

So if no one wants to attack the Lizards to deny them of Acolytes and they can only train, then it seems the Lizard power is quite weak. 

Keep in mind the lizard’s strength comes from two sources: cards in hand and acolytes. If nobody’s attacking them to reduce their pieces, they have the opportunity to spread warriors and gardens across the map, eventually leading to lots of points even if they have no acolytes. The point of the faction is to thrive under peaceful conditions, and only have the opportunity to attack others if they are attacked first - acolytes shouldn’t necessarily be your end goal when playing them.
Got it. Thanks. But it seems quite a passive faction. It cannot battle. It cannot even move if it has no or not enough Acolytes.

thirdman wrote:

Got it. Thanks. But it seems quite a passive faction. It cannot battle. It cannot even move if it has no or not enough Acolytes. 

They don't need to, that's the very idea. They can just appear in your clearings regardless of rule, and can turn your buildings into their own. And when you force them out, you just make them stronger by giving them acolytes.

Anyhow, the lizards don't seem to have quite as much of a tactical agenda as others; instead they have to act on what they are left with by the table a little more than the other factions I'd say.

deranderejan wrote:

Anyhow, the lizards don't seem to have quite as much of a tactical agenda as others; instead they have to act on what they are left with by the table a little more than the other factions I'd say.

Seems you look at the Lizards from a pure mechanical perspective. But their full strenght is levied with power brokering/ deal-making/ table talk.

VariousArtist wrote:

deranderejan wrote:

Anyhow, the lizards don't seem to have quite as much of a tactical agenda as others; instead they have to act on what they are left with by the table a little more than the other factions I'd say.

Seems you look at the Lizards from a pure mechanical perspective. But their full strenght is levied with power brokering/ deal-making/ table talk.

That is true, but isn't it for all factions?

deranderejan wrote:

VariousArtist wrote:

deranderejan wrote:

Anyhow, the lizards don't seem to have quite as much of a tactical agenda as others; instead they have to act on what they are left with by the table a little more than the other factions I'd say.

Seems you look at the Lizards from a pure mechanical perspective. But their full strenght is levied with power brokering/ deal-making/ table talk.

That is true, but isn't it for all factions?

No, some factions are more dependent on this than others. Otters, for example, need table talk more. Moles and coast to victory without talking much more easily. So can the vagabond.
